intro: 97 AI items this week (65 India-relevant) across 9 sources.

items.title: From PGP to Mythos: a brief history of export controls that didn’t stop anyone
items.summary: For the last 30 years, stopping the flow of cybersecurity-related software has proven to be ineffective. It's unclear why it would work now with Anthropic’s cybersecurity model Mythos.

items.title: Critical Copilot vulnerability allowed hackers to steal 2FA code from users
items.summary: SearchLeak exploit shows why the industry's approach to LLM security fails over and over.

items.title: MolmoMotion: Language-guided 3D motion forecasting
The US says ASML’s top chip tool may be in China, but how?
items.summary: There's a commercial logic that cuts against the idea that ASML would risk its export license to arm a Chinese customer.

items.title: MosaicLeaks: Can your research agent keep a secret?
OpenAI launches new initiative to help find and patch open source bugs
items.summary: OpenAI is using AI to help the open source community better protect itself.

items.title: SpaceX inks compute deal with Reflection AI, an open source AI lab
items.summary: Reflection AI will pay $150 million a month beginning July 1, 2026 through 2029 for immediate access to Nvidia's latest GB300 AI chips and supporting hardware across SpaceX's Colossus 2 data center near Memphis, Tennessee.

items.title: PP-OCRv6 on Hugging Face: 50-Language OCR from 1.5M to 34.5M Parameters
From the Hugging Face Hub to robot hardware with Strands Agents and LeRobot
The running list: major tech layoffs in 2026 where employers cited AI
items.summary: A running look — in reverse chronological order — at the bigger tech companies that have announced significant layoffs this year with AI as a stated factor.

items.title: The AI world is getting ‘loopy’
items.summary: The loop takes agentic AI a step further by authorizing a swarm of agents to work continuously in the background, endlessly.

items.title: AI chipmaker Groq confirms $650M raise, re-staffs after Nvidia’s $20B not-acqui-hire deal
items.summary: What does an AI company do after one of those not-acqui-hire deals? Groq raised money, is leaning into its neocloud business, and is hiring new execs.

items.title: Nvidia wants to cut data center water use, but that’s not the same as fixing AI’s water problem
items.summary: Nvidia announced a new cooling system that cuts water use inside the data center. But it does nothing to address AI's biggest water use — fossil fuel power plants.

items.title: Google DeepMind bets $75M on AI’s future in Hollywood with A24 deal
items.summary: Google DeepMind and A24 are teaming up to build AI filmmaking tools.

items.title: Three things to watch amid Anthropic’s latest feud with the government
items.summary: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. For those of you enjoying your summer unaware of Anthropic’s latest feud with the US government, here’s a recap: In April the company said …

items.title: Samsung deploys ChatGPT Enterprise, Codex globally 3 years after AI curbs
items.summary: Samsung Electronics has opened up use of OpenAI's ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex across its global Device Experience division, reversing restrictions imposed in 2023 over data security concerns. The deployment highlights the growing adoption of enterprise AI tools as companies s…

items.title: AI startup Baseten hits $13 billion valuation as Australia's Blackbird makes record bet
items.summary: The round was led by U.S.-based investors ‌Sands Capital ⁠and ⁠Wellington Management, Baseten said late on Monday. Top Australian venture capital ​firm Blackbird VC said it contributed the firm's biggest-ever investment, without ​specifying an amount.

items.title: Qualcomm nearing deal for AI chip startup Modular: report
items.summary: A global supplier of smartphone chips, Qualcomm has been working to ​reduce its reliance on the ⁠volatile handset ‌market by branching out ​into fast-growing ​areas like data center processors ⁠and autonomous vehicle chips.

items.title: Oracle sheds 21,000 employees while ramping up AI investments
Oracle layoffs 2026: Tech giant's headcount shrinks by 21,000 amid rapid AI adoption
items.summary: AI adoption has led to a 13% decline in Oracle's total workforce. The company said in its filing that the workforce adjustments were in ​response to various factors, including management and ⁠product changes, performance ‌issues, strategic shifts and acquisitions.

items.title: As AI adoption grows, token consumption comes under close scrutiny
items.summary: Businesses are facing hefty AI bills, prompting a shift from simply counting AI tokens to scrutinising their actual value. Companies are now tracking cost per outcome and implementing stricter controls like usage dashboards and approval mechanisms. Firms are exploring cheaper …

items.title: Software-hardware loop fuelling all new AI super cycle
items.summary: While energy and cooling challenges persist, a lot more hardware requirements are expected to emerge, Synopsys CIO Sriram Sitaraman told ET. While energy and cooling pose challenges, innovation is anticipated to overcome these. Enterprises are also seeing productivity boosts f…

items.title: A new bunch of AI spin-offs ripe for the picking
items.summary: Indian tech unicorns are transforming their internal AI tools into independent businesses, mirroring successes like Amazon Web Services and Slack. Companies such as NoBroker, Moglix, Apna, and Practo are leveraging years of accumulated data and domain expertise to launch AI-po…

careers.note: Demand snapshot for 2026. ML Engineer is the most in-demand AI title; agentic-AI and deep-learning skills carry the highest pay premiums; Python + one cloud is the baseline. Specialisation beats generalisation for freshers.

paths.note: #1 AI title — build, deploy & maintain models in production. Best all-round entry into AI.
RAG, tool-use & multi-agent systems. ~40% of enterprise apps will embed AI agents by year-end.
Neural nets for vision & speech. Highest premiums; usually needs strong maths / grad study.
Ship & scale models on the cloud. Cloud AI certs add 20–25% to salary.
Foundational, widest entry door; a reliable first step into AI.
Imaging, robotics, autonomous systems, medical AI.
Powers chatbots, search & agents — the hottest application area.
5–8 yrs experience; $140k–$332k bands. Translates AI into business value.
Alignment, evals, red-teaming & policy — newer but expanding fast.

fresher.why: The lingua franca of ML — non-negotiable.
Linear algebra, probability & calculus — enough to reason about models.
Regression, classification, evaluation, the ML workflow.
Build & train neural nets; PyTorch dominates research & jobs.
Ship a small LLM app — the most visible skill to recruiters now.
Real AI work is mostly data; SQL is everywhere.
AWS leads (~40% of postings), then Azure & GCP. Version everything.
A deployed project that solves a real problem is the #1 differentiator.

experienced.why: Docker, CI/CD, monitoring, model/versioning — production is where value lives.
Multi-agent orchestration, tool-use, evals — the surging frontier.
AWS/Azure/GCP architecture; certs add a 20–25% salary premium.
Adapt, measure and harden models for a domain.
Cost & latency engineering for large models.
Trade-offs across data, latency, cost & reliability at scale.
CV, NLP, recsys, healthcare — depth beats breadth at senior levels.
Leading teams and tying models to measurable outcomes.

profile.name: Harman Singh Sandhu
profile.blurb: I build AI/ML projects and write about them. This site is one of them — an automated daily AI digest.

repos.name: ai-weekly-report
repos.desc: My personalized AI newsletter that runs on an agentic AI

repos.name: Ace-rentals
repos.desc: Ace Rentals: A full-stack property rental platform built with Node.js, Express, and MongoDB. Features user authentication, image uploads, geocoding, reviews, and secure account management. Inspired by Airbnb.
